issues.title: API v1 router pathMatches function is referenced but never defined
issues.description: The resolveRequiredScope function calls pathMatches() and normalize() but pathMatches is never defined in the file. This will cause a runtime crash when any API route resolution is attempted, breaking the entire webhook and integration layer.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/v1/router.ts, in the resolveRequiredScope function: add the missing pathMatches helper function. BEFORE: the function references pathMatches() which does not exist, causing ReferenceError at runtime. AFTER: add this function before resolveRequiredScope: function pathMatches(pattern: string, path: string): boolean { const patternParts = pattern.split('/'); const pathParts = path.split('/'); if (patternParts.length !== pathParts.length) return false; return patternParts.every((part, i) => part.startsWith(':') || part === pathParts[i]); } This enables route matching with :param segments. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: App shell missing ARIA landmarks and skip navigation target
issues.description: The SkipToContent component is imported but the main content area lacks an id='main-content' target and role='main' landmark. Screen reader users cannot skip navigation. The sidebar also lacks role='navigation' and aria-label.
issues.prompt: In the file src/App.tsx, in the main layout wrapper that renders the Sidebar and page content: add ARIA landmarks to the content area. BEFORE: the main content div has no id or role attributes, so SkipToContent links to nowhere and screen readers cannot identify the main region. AFTER: 1. On the div wrapping the route content (the element adjacent to Sidebar), add id='main-content' and role='main' and aria-label='Page content'. 2. Ensure the Sidebar component wrapper has role='navigation' and aria-label='Main navigation'. 3. Add a <LiveRegion> component after the RouteAnnouncer if not already present to announce route changes. This ensures the skip link works and screen readers can navigate between landmarks. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: createScopedClient proxy does not handle storage or auth property access
issues.description: The Proxy in createScopedClient only intercepts the 'from' property. Accessing supabase.storage, supabase.auth, or supabase.functions through the scoped client falls through to Reflect.get but the proxy type assertion means TypeScript thinks it returns DbClient methods. This causes silent failures when edge function calls or file uploads use the scoped client.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/client.ts, in the createScopedClient function: ensure the Proxy passes through non-'from' property access correctly. BEFORE: the Proxy only has a handler for prop === 'from' and all other property access (auth, storage, functions, channel, removeChannel) falls through without explicit handling, which works at runtime via default Reflect.get but is fragile. AFTER: add explicit passthrough in the get handler: if (prop !== 'from') { return Reflect.get(target, prop, receiver); } before the 'from' handler block. Also add a comment: '// Only intercept .from() for project scoping. All other Supabase client methods (auth, storage, functions, realtime) pass through unmodified.' This makes the intent explicit and prevents future regressions if proxy behavior changes. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: MobileLayout imported but no responsive breakpoint gate in App shell
issues.description: MobileLayout is imported and useMediaQuery hook exists but the App component does not conditionally render the mobile layout versus desktop layout based on screen width. Mobile users see the desktop sidebar instead of bottom navigation, violating field-first design standards requiring 44px touch targets and bottom nav on mobile.
issues.prompt: In the file src/App.tsx, in the main App component or the layout wrapper inside HashRouter: add a responsive layout gate using the useMediaQuery hook. BEFORE: MobileLayout is imported but never used in the render tree; mobile users see the full desktop Sidebar which is unusable on 375px screens. AFTER: 1. At the top of the component body (inside the router), add: const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 768px)'); 2. Wrap the layout conditional: if isMobile, render <MobileLayout>{routeContent}</MobileLayout> instead of the Sidebar + content div. 3. Ensure MobileLayout receives children and renders them inside a container with id='main-content' role='main'. This gives mobile users bottom navigation and touch-friendly layout per the design standards. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Scoped client cache has no eviction, leaks memory on project switching
issues.description: The scopedClientCache Map grows to SCOPED_CLIENT_CACHE_MAX but never evicts old entries. The LRU refresh logic deletes and re-inserts on hit but the insertion path never checks size or evicts when at capacity. Users who switch between 6+ projects accumulate stale proxy objects indefinitely.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/client.ts, in the createScopedClient function: add LRU eviction when the cache exceeds SCOPED_CLIENT_CACHE_MAX. BEFORE: the cache grows unbounded because entries are only added (scopedClientCache.set) but never removed when size exceeds SCOPED_CLIENT_CACHE_MAX (5). The constant is defined but unused for eviction. AFTER: after the line 'scopedClientCache.set(projectId, proxy)' (at the end of the function before returning), add eviction logic: if (scopedClientCache.size > SCOPED_CLIENT_CACHE_MAX) { const oldestKey = scopedClientCache.keys().next().value; if (oldestKey !== undefined) { scopedClientCache.delete(oldestKey); } } This ensures the oldest entry (first inserted, per Map iteration order) is evicted when the cache is full, preventing memory leaks during multi-project workflows. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's API layer has full OpenAPI documentation, versioned endpoints, and rate limiting middleware built into every route. SiteSync's v1 router is a static registry with a broken path matcher — it cannot actually dispatch requests. The error handling hierarchy (ApiError, NetworkError, etc.) is well structured and ahead of Fieldwire's basic error strings, but the scoped client proxy has subtle bugs that would cause data leakage in multi-project scenarios. The mobile layout gate is completely missing from the App shell, meaning field crews get a desktop UI — the exact failure mode that makes Procore's mobile app feel like an adapted desktop app. Fixing the 5 issues above closes the gap on infrastructure reliability before building P0 features.
summary: Infrastructure scores 57 overall, held back by a broken API route matcher (pathMatches undefined), missing mobile layout gate (MobileLayout imported but never rendered), and missing ARIA landmarks (skip link targets nowhere). The scoped Supabase client proxy is architecturally sound but has a memory leak in its LRU cache and fragile passthrough for non-from properties. TypeScript quality is the strongest dimension at 72 thanks to well-typed error hierarchy and query key factories. The v1 router pattern is enterprise-grade in design but non-functional due to the missing helper. Priority for this cycle: fix the pathMatches crash, wire up mobile layout, add ARIA landmarks, patch the scoped client cache eviction, and make proxy passthrough explicit. These five fixes unblock the P0 roadmap items (email notifications, schedule import) which depend on working API routing and mobile-first rendering.
